column3: An individual (historic or modern) acting in research, conservation, authorship, etc.

This model identifies a person with PIDs, labels, and descriptions; records name parts and honorifics, external identifiers and web presence, research disciplines, life dates, affiliations, and optional contact details and residence.
An organisation or group the person is/was affiliated with or a member of.
A place where the person lives/lived.
Documents that discuss or evidence the person (CVs, bios, authority files).
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
Documents that discuss or evidence the person (CVs, bios, authority files).
The person's given/first name as an appellation.
The person's family/last name as an appellation.
Honorifics or titles (e.g., Dr, Prof, Sir).
Any other name the person is known by, including former names.
A globally resolvable identifier for the person (e.g., Wikidata QID, ISNI).
Additional identifiers from authority sources, use controlled terms e.g.: ORCID, VIAF, ISNI, Wikidata, ResearchGate, Google Scholar.
A personal or institutional profile page URL (e.g., university staff page, ResearchGate, Academia.edu).
Contact information for the person; email address or telephone number.

This 'area of work' can be a simple or complex event, it purpose here is to provide a mechanism to record simple specialities of research discipline as person has experience in or is known for. For professional field(s) or domain(s) of practice, use controlled terms e.g.: paintings conservation, heritage science, analytical chemistry.
